title: quercus × coutinhoi subsp. duriensis
intro: Quercus × coutinhoi subsp. duriensis, also known as Cork Oak, is a hybrid of Quercus robur and Quercus faginea, a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The bark and leaves of the plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus × coutinhoi subsp. duriensis include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
